topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The accusation landed in the sterile quiet of the faculty lounge like a dropped stone. I stared at Professor Albright, his expression a carefully constructed mask of disappointment. "The similarities are undeniable, Mark," he stated, tapping the printed page with a ruler. My hands, gripping my lukewarm coffee mug, felt suddenly clumsy. I looked down at my notes, the ink blurring under the harsh fluorescent lights. *This can't be right,* I thought, a slow, heavy settling in my chest. I’d spent weeks wrestling with the concepts, synthesizing ideas in my own clumsy way. To have someone claim that was theft felt like a profound injustice, a complete misreading of the effort I’d poured in. I opened my mouth, but only a dry rasp emerged.

[story 2]

The summons felt like a physical weight pressing down on my shoulders. I walked into the office, the carpet muffling my steps, and sat across the desk from the Dean. He spoke in measured tones, laying out the evidence with an air of unwavering certainty. Every word he uttered seemed to build a wall between me and any possibility of defense. I kept my gaze fixed on the intricate pattern of the rug beneath his polished shoes. A tightness began to coil in my stomach, a cold, prickly sensation spreading outwards. I didn't argue; I just watched him, cataloging the slight tremor in his hand as he adjusted his glasses. *They don't see the process,* I realized, a bitter taste rising at the back of my throat.

[story 3]

When the email arrived, the sheer audacity of the charge made me pause before clicking 'open.' It was a blunt instrument, designed to shut down conversation before it even began. I reread the allegations three times, my eyes scanning the carefully chosen vocabulary. A faint, almost imperceptible shake ran through my jaw. I leaned back in my chair, the leather groaning beneath me, and let a long silence stretch between the notification and my reply. I felt a strange detachment, as if watching a play from the wings. *This narrative doesn't fit the reality,* I concluded, typing a response that was deliberately calm, devoid of the frantic energy I felt bubbling just beneath the surface.
